html:
5-step camera translation
Full diagram: five camera frustums translated right by equal steps.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: A horizontal path line indicating the lateral trajectory of the camera
Five sequential camera wireframe frustums arranged side-by-side along the lateral path
Three minimalist landmark points representing Near (Z1), Mid (Z2), and Far (Z3) depth targets
Projection lines (thin dashed lines) linking each landmark to the optical centers of the sequential cameras
Minimalist projection planes (flat rectangles) attached to each camera showing the projected point positions
Flow vectors (small directional arrows) on the projection planes showing the relative velocity/displacement
A clean baseline grid representing depth planes (Z values)

interactions.label: Show 5-Pose Sequence
interactions.teaches: Toggles between a single moving camera and a static side-by-side sequence of 5 camera poses, matching the standard textbook diagram layout

interactions.label: Lateral Velocity (Vx)
interactions.teaches: Controls the distance/velocity step between the sequential camera poses, scaling the magnitude of the resulting image displacements

interactions.label: Interactive Target Depth (Z)
interactions.teaches: Directly adjusts the depth of the nearest target point, allowing the user to watch the projection lines change angle and compress as depth increases

interactions.label: Focal Length (f)
interactions.teaches: Varies the sensor distance from the pinhole, scaling the size and motion vectors of the projected points

demo_steps.title: Minimalist lateral translation sequence
demo_steps.narration: Here we see five sequential camera poses moving from left to right. Notice how the projection lines map the target points onto each camera's sensor. The visual style is kept strictly black-and-white and minimalist to emphasize pure geometric relationships.

demo_steps.title: The 1/Z depth parallax effect
demo_steps.narration: Observe the three targets at different depths. The nearest target has highly angled projection lines that shift rapidly across the five sensors. The farthest target's lines are nearly parallel, meaning its projected position barely moves—this is the 1/Z parallax effect in action.

demo_steps.title: Varying camera velocity
demo_steps.narration: Increasing the lateral velocity spreads the five camera poses further apart. This increases the displacement of the projected points between adjacent frames, making the motion vectors longer. The ratio of displacement between near and far objects remains strictly proportional to 1/Z.

demo_steps.title: Focal length scaling
demo_steps.narration: Adjusting the focal length pushes the virtual sensors further from the camera origins. This magnifies the projected coordinates, showing why focal length directly scales the apparent motion field in the image plane.

plan.camera_suggestion: Elevated perspective view from the front-right, looking down at the camera path and the depth targets, allowing clear visualization of the depth planes and projection lines simultaneously.
plan.notes: Ensure a clean, high-contrast black-and-white line-art look. The wireframe cameras should use thin solid lines for the frustum and dashed lines for projection rays. If 'Show 5-Pose Sequence' is turned off, animate a single camera sliding smoothly back and forth along the path while updating the projection lines dynamically.

elements: 3D Camera model representing a pinhole and image sensor frustum
Camera path indicator (horizontal line for lateral motion, longitudinal line for forward motion)
Three distinct 3D landmarks at varying depths: a near tree (Z = 3), a midground house (Z = 7), and a far mountain/cloud (Z = 20)
Pinhole projection rays connecting the 3D landmarks through the camera lens center to the image plane
Virtual 2D view finder screen showing the projected landmarks as 2D symbols
Velocity vectors (flow arrows) overlaying the projected points on the 2D screen, scaled to represent image-plane velocity (x_dot, y_dot)
Dynamic vector annotations displaying the calculated formula values: velocity = -f * V / Z

interactions.label: Camera Motion Type
interactions.teaches: Switches between lateral translation (causing parallel parallax flows) and forward translation (producing a radial expansion from the Focus of Expansion)

interactions.label: Camera Velocity (V)
interactions.teaches: Adjusting camera speed scales the magnitude of the 2D optical flow vectors directly and changes the motion direction

interactions.label: Focal Length (f)
interactions.teaches: Increasing the focal length acts as a magnifier, scaling up both the projected size of the scene and the optical flow speeds proportionally

interactions.label: Highlight 1/Z Depth layers
interactions.teaches: Isolates and highlights individual depth planes to visually clarify why the flow magnitude is inversely proportional to Z

demo_steps.title: Lateral translation & optical flow
demo_steps.narration: The camera is translating laterally to the right. Notice how the projected points on the virtual screen move to the left. The flow vectors are in the exact opposite direction of the camera movement, which is mathematically represented by the negative sign in the velocity equation.

demo_steps.title: The 1/Z parallax effect
demo_steps.narration: By highlighting the depth layers, we can see the parallax effect in action. The green vector on the near tree (Z=3) is large and fast. The yellow vector on the house (Z=7) is slower. The distant cloud (Z=20) has a vector near zero, appearing almost stationary—an exact demonstration of the inverse 1/Z scaling.

demo_steps.title: Increasing translation speed
demo_steps.narration: When the camera speeds up, the flow velocity vectors grow proportionally across all layers. However, the ratio of movement speed between the near tree and the far mountain remains strictly tied to their relative depths.

demo_steps.title: Focal length scaling
demo_steps.narration: Increasing the camera's focal length to f=2.6 zooms in on the scene, making the objects larger on our viewfinder. Because of this magnification, the apparent motion across the sensor is also faster, stretching the optical flow vectors.

demo_steps.title: Forward motion & FOE
demo_steps.narration: Let's switch to forward motion. The camera now translates along its optical axis. Instead of parallel vectors, the motion field expands radially outwards from a single central point: the Focus of Expansion (FOE). Notice that the FOE itself has zero optical flow.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Perspective view from a front-right elevated vantage point. This orientation allows the viewer to simultaneously see the 3D scene depth layers, the physical translation of the camera body, and the 2D viewfinder display projecting the optical flow vectors.
interactionPlan.notes: Create a split screen or floating picture-in-picture viewport representing the camera's internal viewfinder. Color-code the three depth layers (e.g., Green for Near Tree, Yellow for Mid House, Blue/White for Far Cloud) and match those colors on the 3D geometry, projected points, and flow vector arrows to make the 1/Z relationship immediately intuitive.

attempts.html:
Optical flow from camera translation
Lateral translation & optical flow
The camera is translating laterally to the right. Projected points move in the opposite direction.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The source figure contains only a sequence of 5 black-and-white camera icons translating horizontally with red arrows, whereas the generated figure contains colorful 3D landmarks (tree, house, cloud), a ground grid, and a virtual 2D viewfinder.
The original figure has absolutely no text or mathematical labels, whereas the generated code has many floating HTML labels and equations.
The sequence of 5 translation steps shown side-by-side in the source is replaced with an interactive single-camera timeline controlled by 'Step 1' to 'Step 5' buttons.
evaluation.notes: The generated scene is an extremely rich, high-quality educational demo of optical flow, but it is highly unfaithful to the extremely minimalist, 5-step camera translation diagram of the original source figure.
action_items: Simplify the visual elements dramatically to match the minimalist black-and-white aesthetic of the original diagram.
Create a toggle or view that shows the 5 sequential camera poses side-by-side (or in sequence) as illustrated in the source, rather than just one active camera with heavy landmark models.
Remove or make optional the extra 3D objects (tree, house, cloud) and equations to avoid clutter and stay closer to the original diagram's simplicity.
Adjust the camera wireframe rendering to more closely match the specific 2D/3D projection line style of the original cameras.

feedback.rationale: The overall interaction structure and visual complexity are fundamentally misaligned with the source, representing a planning failure that hallucinated complex landmarks and an interactive timeline instead of a simple 5-camera side-by-side sequence.
action_items: Simplify the visual elements dramatically to match the minimalist black-and-white aesthetic of the original diagram.
Create a toggle or view that shows the 5 sequential camera poses side-by-side (or in sequence) as illustrated in the source, rather than just one active camera with heavy landmark models.
Remove or make optional the extra 3D objects (tree, house, cloud) and equations to avoid clutter and stay closer to the original diagram's simplicity.
Adjust the camera wireframe rendering to more closely match the specific 2D/3D projection line style of the original cameras.
actionItems: Simplify the visual elements dramatically to match the minimalist black-and-white aesthetic of the original diagram.
Create a toggle or view that shows the 5 sequential camera poses side-by-side (or in sequence) as illustrated in the source, rather than just one active camera with heavy landmark models.
Remove or make optional the extra 3D objects (tree, house, cloud) and equations to avoid clutter and stay closer to the original diagram's simplicity.
Adjust the camera wireframe rendering to more closely match the specific 2D/3D projection line style of the original cameras.

attempts.html:
5-step camera translation
Full source diagram: five identical camera snapshots, each preceded by a rightward translation arrow.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The camera vector icons are slightly more elongated vertically than the original's squat look.
The red arrows are thinner and have a different aspect ratio compared to the blocky original arrows.
The illustration is strictly 2D flat, lacking 3D depth.
evaluation.notes: Excellent interactive sequencing, but translates a purely 2D diagram flatly into Three.js without leveraging 3D depth.
action_items: Incorporate 3D camera models and a 3D translation path instead of flat 2D shapes.
Make the red translation arrows thicker to match the original's bold style.
Add 3D text labels showing translation coordinates or step numbers inside the viewport.

feedback.rationale: The interactive sequencing and concept are highly rated, but the execution needs refinement to replace the flat 2D shapes with proper 3D models and thicker arrows as suggested.
action_items: Incorporate 3D camera models and a 3D translation path instead of flat 2D shapes.
Make the red translation arrows thicker to match the original's bold style.
Add 3D text labels showing translation coordinates or step numbers inside the viewport.

Make the red translation arrows thicker to match the original's bold style.
Add 3D text labels showing translation coordinates or step numbers inside the viewport.

attempts.html:
5-step camera translation
Full diagram: five camera frustums translated right by equal steps.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The original figure is a simple 2D diagram, while the generated version adds 3D depth, a grid floor, and a 'depth' dimension arrow.
Labels 'P1' to 'P5' are added below the cameras, which are not present in the original diagram.
The camera models in the generated version are represented as 3D boxes with lines to a pinhole, whereas the original has flat 2D frustums.
An extra 'depth' label and blue arrow are added to the left side.
evaluation.notes: The generated figure translates a simple 2D translation diagram into an interactive 3D sequence, adding extra labels and depth cues not present in the original.
action_items: Remove the 'depth' cue arrow and label to match the simplicity of the original figure.
Add an option to toggle between 2D orthographic flat view (matching the original) and the 3D perspective view.
Remove the 'P1' to 'P5' labels as they are not in the source textbook illustration.
Simplify the camera frustum geometry to look closer to the line-art style of the original.

feedback.rationale: The conceptual model and interactivity are highly rated, but the implementation needs refinement to remove hallucinated labels and simplify the 3D geometry to better align with the original 2D source style.
